name: sidecar
description: /sidecar <args> — thin wrapper over the `sidecar` marketplace CLI (host-local, on PATH via `hx install sidecar`). Verbs — init (scaffold project.tape + CLAUDE.md symlink + LATTICE_POLICY.md) · sync (pull marketplace + refresh cache + installed_plugins.json + enable plugins + mirror commands) · mirror (refresh ~/.claude/commands/ mirrors so plugin commands are bare-invocable — `Skill("imagine")` instead of `Skill("imagine:imagine")` — auto-runs at sync tail) · shadow [plan] (drop the FORCED `plugin:command` namespace #15882 — mirror+disable every command/skill plugin, splicing SKILL.md triggers into the bare command so the bare form stays lossless; hook/mcp plugins kept enabled; `plan` = dry-run; re-asserted at sync tail; revert with unshadow) · unshadow (re-enable the shadowed plugins — namespaced again — and drop created skill mirrors) · paths (0.6.0 NEW — manage the LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST `~/.sidecar/local-paths`; pool-route 0.10.0 forces any Bash cmd whose argv contains a `/`-prefixed token starting with a registered prefix to run LOCAL. Sub-forms: `paths` lists, `paths add` registers cwd, `paths add <dir>` registers an abs-resolved dir (refuses non-existent / already-present), `paths rm <prefix>` removes a line by exact match. Seeded on first use with `~/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/` + `~/core/sidecar/` so sidecar's own hexa hooks stop bouncing into the pool. Atomic writes via tmp+mv; comments and blank lines preserved on rm; the whitelist itself IS the opt-in — no env opt-out — @D s11 safe; 0.7.0 add/rm are SIGN-GATED (key `paths`: `! sidecar sign paths` first) since the list overrides pool-route.) · reap (0.7.0 NEW — reap stale `hexa-lsp` servers idle > 2h; dry-run by default, `reap --kill` terminates; never touches claude (@D s9) or active hexa runs) · handoff (0.8.0 NEW — the cross-project handoff registry, single SSOT for cross-repo gap/patch/bug/RFC handoffs (commons g11·g36·g48·g59·g60; retires the per-repo INBOX domain). `handoff add <repo> <text...>` appends an open JSONL entry to ~/.sidecar/handoff/handoff.jsonl, `handoff ls [<repo>]` renders a table (filter by repo), `handoff done <id>` flips an entry's status → done, bare `handoff` prints a counts summary. JSONL not markdown (structured carrier per project.tape s2); store is repo-external runtime state, portable via $HOME; atomic writes via tmp+rename) · sign (user sign-off gate). `sign <key>` mints a 5-min token at ~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign that unlocks agent edits to a sign-gated governance SSOT file — the sign-guard hook hard-denies those edits until a fresh token exists; keys are `commons` (commons.tape), `project` (project.tape), `gitignore` (.gitignore), and `paths` (~/.sidecar/local-paths). Bare `sign` / `sign --list` shows active tokens; `sign clear [<key>]` removes them. Multi-user enable profiles — `profile [<name>]` shows or sets the enable profile (minimal = core only · hexa = core + hexa-lang · full = everything, the default · master = full + creator-only plugins), `enable`/`disable <plugin>` force one plugin on/off as a per-plugin override, `reset <plugin>` drops the override so it follows the profile; each re-applies the install. `master [on|off|status]` mints/removes the ~/.sidecar/master creator marker — a `master`-tier plugin enables ONLY while that marker exists (force-disabled otherwise, even under full/master profile; a per-plugin override still escape-hatches). Profile state lives in ~/.sidecar/profile + ~/.sidecar/plugin-overrides.json; install.hexa enables a plugin per .claude-plugin/profiles.json tiers (plugin disable, not an in-guard opt-out — @D s11 safe). Args pass through unchanged.
